objections.description: Loading React + ReactDOM + Babel from CDN and transpiling JSX in-browser is not production-grade. It adds ~1MB of Babel standalone to every page load, introduces runtime parsing overhead, blocks first paint, and creates a hard dependency on third-party CDN availability. This directly contradicts the performance guardrail of perceived-first-paint under 1.5s. A production frontend must ship pre-built assets.
objections.suggested_fix: Add a minimal build step using Vite (or esbuild directly) with output to src/frontend/web/dist. Vite with React plugin produces optimized ESM bundles in <2s build time. web_server.py serves the dist/ directory as static files. This keeps the repo self-contained, requires only `npm run build`, and eliminates runtime transpilation. If the team truly cannot accept Node in CI, use esbuild as a single binary with zero npm dependencies.

objections.description: Component list is flat with no file/directory structure, no clear ownership boundaries, and no explanation of composition hierarchy. 'AppShell' containing everything is not architecture — it's a God component waiting to happen. There's no error boundary strategy, no loading state machine, and no specification of which components are routes vs. shared vs. domain-specific.
objections.suggested_fix: Define explicit directory structure: src/frontend/app/{shell,routing}, src/frontend/features/{search,timeline,document}, src/frontend/shared/{ui,hooks,tokens}. Each feature directory owns its screen component, sub-components, and data-fetching hook. Specify composition: AppShell > Router > Screen > [feature components]. Add React error boundaries at the screen level. Define a state machine for each screen: idle | loading | error | empty | populated.

objections.description: 'Top-level app store via React hooks' is dangerously vague. This will either become a single useState blob that causes full-tree re-renders on every keystroke, or an ad-hoc mess of prop drilling. For a search-centric app with autocomplete, debounced input, selected document state, and theme toggling, you need to be explicit about state boundaries.
objections.suggested_fix: Define three explicit contexts with separate providers to avoid unnecessary re-renders: (1) ThemeContext (dark/light, persisted to localStorage), (2) SearchContext (query, results, autocomplete suggestions, loading state — isolated so typing doesn't re-render the dashboard), (3) DocumentContext (selected doc ID, document data, panel open state). Use useReducer for SearchContext since it has multiple related state transitions. Specify that autocomplete uses a debounced fetch (300ms) with AbortController to cancel stale requests.

objections.description: Client-side routing is specified but no router implementation is named. Without React Router or a custom solution, you'll end up with conditional rendering based on string state, which breaks browser back/forward, deep linking, and URL sharing. This is a production app, not a demo.
objections.suggested_fix: Either use a lightweight hash-based router (since web_server serves index.html for all paths anyway, or configure it to do so), or implement a minimal custom router with pushState/popState (~50 lines). Define the route map explicitly: / = Dashboard, /search?q= = Search results, /document/:id = Timeline/Detail, /document/:id/action = Action sheet. Ensure web_server.py has a catch-all route that serves index.html for any path not matching /api/*.

objections.description: Token system mentions categories (typography, spacing, motion, elevation, status colors) but defines zero actual values. Without concrete tokens, the other agents (Qwen especially) cannot evaluate visual hierarchy or accessibility compliance. You can't approve what doesn't exist.
objections.suggested_fix: Include the actual token definitions in the spec. Minimum: type scale (5-7 steps with rem values and line-heights), spacing scale (4px base, 8 steps), color palette (background, surface, text-primary/secondary/tertiary, accent, error, success for both light and dark), elevation (3 levels using box-shadow with dark-mode-appropriate values), motion durations (fast: 120ms, normal: 200ms, slow: 350ms) with cubic-bezier curves (not ease or linear per requirements — use something like cubic-bezier(0.2, 0, 0, 1) for deceleration).

objections.description: Migration path says 'switch index.html to new shell after approval, retain old files only until parity is verified' but doesn't specify how parity is verified, who approves, or what rollback looks like. This is a flag-day migration hiding behind vague language.
objections.suggested_fix: Concrete migration: (1) New React app builds to src/frontend/web/dist/. (2) Add a route in web_server.py: /v2 serves new index.html, / continues serving old. (3) Define parity checklist: search works, autocomplete works, document detail loads, dark/light toggle works, all endpoints return data, mobile layout verified at 375px. (4) After parity confirmed, swap / to serve new app, move old files to src/frontend/web/_legacy/. (5) Remove _legacy after one release cycle.

objections.description: Swipe affordances and pull-to-refresh in a React SPA without a library are significant implementation complexity. Pull-to-refresh in particular requires touch event handling, scroll position detection, rubber-band physics, and careful interaction with browser's native pull-to-refresh on mobile Chrome/Safari. This is easily a week of work to get right and is not core to a search application.
objections.suggested_fix: Drop pull-to-refresh from v1 scope entirely. For swipe affordances on list rows, spec the exact interaction: what happens on swipe-left vs swipe-right, what's the threshold (40% of row width), what's the snap-back animation. If the answer is 'we don't know yet,' cut it from v1 and add a manual refresh button instead.

objections.description: Performance guardrails mention 'lazy render lower-priority panels' but don't define what's lower-priority. Also missing: bundle size budget, image/SVG strategy, cache headers from web_server, and whether the API responses are cacheable.
objections.suggested_fix: Define: (1) Critical render path = app shell + search bar + top searches. Everything below the fold is lazy. (2) Bundle budget: <150KB gzipped total JS including React. (3) SVG icons inlined as React components, not fetched. (4) web_server.py sets Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000 on hashed assets, no-cache on index.html. (5) API responses: cache /api/top-searches and /api/search-examples for 5min in a service worker or stale-while-revalidate.

objections.description: Acceptance criteria restates the convergence criteria from the task prompt without adding specificity. 'All four agents approve' is a process statement, not a testable acceptance criterion.
objections.suggested_fix: Add measurable criteria: Lighthouse accessibility score >= 95, color contrast ratios meet WCAG AA (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text), all interactive elements have focus-visible styles, tab order is logical, screen reader can navigate all screens, no layout shift on theme toggle, animations respect prefers-reduced-motion, works without JS for initial content (or explicitly state it doesn't and why that's acceptable for this app).

suggestions.description: Visual direction says 'dark-mode-first' but the token system should define both themes simultaneously with dark as default. Implement via a `data-theme` attribute on <html> rather than a class toggle — this makes CSS selectors cleaner: `[data-theme='light']` overrides custom properties. Store preference in localStorage and check `prefers-color-scheme` as fallback on first visit.

Add an explicit API client layer. Don't scatter fetch() calls across components. Create src/frontend/app/api.js that exports typed functions: searchDocuments(query), getAutocomplete(query), getDocument(id), getTopSearches(), getSearchExamples(). Each function handles errors consistently, returns structured results, and is the single place to add auth headers, base URL config, or request interceptors later.

The spec mentions no keyboard accessibility strategy. For a search-centric app: Cmd/Ctrl+K should focus search, Escape should dismiss bottom sheets/action sheets, arrow keys should navigate autocomplete results, Enter should select. Define these upfront — retrofitting keyboard nav is 3x the work.

No mention of loading/error/empty states per screen. Each of the 3 key screens needs all four states designed: skeleton (loading), populated (happy path), empty (no results/no data), error (network failure/500). The empty states especially need distinct copy and CTAs — 'No results for X, try Y' is a core search UX concern.
